Environmental Impact Statement, Historic Hill Urban Renewal Area, Newport, Rhode Island

The Historic Hill Urban Renewal Project in Newport, Rhode Island, aims to revitalize a deteriorated area by improving infrastructure, preserving historic buildings, and providing new housing. The project will involve demolishing some structures, increasing traffic on major roads, and changing the character of certain streets. It is designed to eliminate blight and enhance the community's aesthetic and social environment. The project will displace some families, but relocation assistance is available. The community hopes the project will bring positive environmental and social impacts.
U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
City of Newport, Office of the Mayor

The Historic Hill Urban Renewal Project was proposed to address the decline of a significant area in Newport, aiming to revitalize it through infrastructure improvements and historic preservation. While the project had support for its potential benefits, there was also public concern regarding the displacement of families and changes to the neighborhood's character. Ultimately, the project proceeded with plans for relocation assistance to affected residents.
"We feel that this Project will have a positive environmental impact and should be approved as soon as possible." — Humphrey J. Donnelly, Mayor

"The plan for the Historic Hill Urban Renewal Project of Newport is an important one and includes many excellent elements which if successfully executed will safeguard much of the historic character of the old part of this city." — Rhode Island Historical Preservation Commission
